channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Supreme Court says third language should begin earlier than Class IX to avoid burden on board exam students
item.description: The Supreme Court observed that introducing a third language only from Class IX could place an unnecessary academic burden on students who are already preparing for their board examinations. The remar...

item.title: Respected Community Leader Mary Amaechi Dies at 89
item.description: The family of former Rivers State Governor and African Democratic Congress (ADC) vice-presidential candidate Rotimi Amaechi announced the peaceful passing of his mother, Mary Amaechi, at the age of 89...

item.title: Aldi settles underpayment claims with $50 million payout
item.description: The article reports that Aldi, the German supermarket chain, has agreed to pay over $50 million to workers who were underpaid during their employment. The settlement comes after investigations reveale...

item.title: Vancouver Whitecaps vs. Chicago Fire match postponed over hazardous air quality
item.description: The Vancouver Whitecaps FC's scheduled match against the Chicago Fire at Soldier Field has been rescheduled for Tuesday, October 6, due to hazardous wildfire smoke affecting air quality in the region....

item.title: Metrorail commuter harassed by security guards for not greeting on Cape Town train
item.description: Lumka Mbele, a Metrorail commuter and influencer, shared a harrowing experience of being physically assaulted by security guards on a Cape Town train after refusing to reciprocate a flirtatious greeti...

item.title: Andy Serkis Addresses Criticism Over All-White Cast of New 'Lord of the Rings' Film
item.description: The upcoming standalone film 'The Hunt for Gollum,' part of the 'Lord of the Rings' franchise, has faced backlash over its all-white cast. Director Andy Serkis acknowledged Tolkien's Norse influences ...

item.title: Tanzania calls for SADC unity to address global security and economic threats
item.description: Tanzania has called upon Southern African Development Community (SADC) member states to prioritize regional unity in addressing emerging global challenges that threaten both security and economic stab...

item.title: Bea Alonzo and Vincent Co's Wedding: A Look at the Tarot Vlog That Gained Attention
item.description: The article discusses the viral trend surrounding Bea Alonzo's old tarot reading vlog, which gained renewed attention after her wedding to Vincent Co. Published on July 16, 2026, the piece highlights ...

item.title: White House aide faces leave after betting on Trump's speeches
item.description: Gabriel Perez, a long-time technical assistant to Donald Trump, has been placed on unpaid administrative leave after allegations he bet over $US100,000 on prediction markets based on Trump's speeches....

item.title: Laura Wolvaardt and Aiden Markram dominate CSA Awards 2026
item.description: Laura Wolvaardt solidified her status as South Africa's premier female cricketer by sweeping all women's categories at the CSA Professional Awards, including Player of the Year, best ODI player, best ...

item.title: Critique of U.S. Defense Spending and the Military-Industrial Complex
item.description: The article critiques the U.S. defense budget, highlighting its unsustainable growth and the role of the Military-Industrial-Congressional Complex (MICC) in perpetuating excessive military spending. I...

item.title: Kano Hisbah enforces ban on mobile movie distribution to safeguard Islamic values
item.description: The Kano State Hisbah Board has enforced a ban on downloading and distributing movies through mobile phones across the state's 44 local government areas. The directive, announced by Deputy Commander G...

item.title: Labour appoints Sadiq Khan and 25 others as new House of Lords peers
item.description: London Mayor Sir Sadiq Khan has been appointed as one of 26 new peers entering the House of Lords, marking one of Sir Keir Starmer's final acts as prime minister. The nominations, announced ahead of S...

item.title: Real Estate Experts Weigh In on Buying and Selling Amid Economic Uncertainty
item.description: The article explores current real estate market dynamics, emphasizing that most of Ontario and British Columbia are buyer's markets due to slower sales and price reductions. Experts highlight that whi...

item.title: Gal Gadot's New Film 'The Runner' Explores Legal Drama and Maternal Struggle
item.description: The article discusses the trailer of the upcoming film 'The Runner,' starring Gal Gadot as a lawyer racing against time to save her son. Directed by Kevin Macdonald, the thriller also features Damian ...

item.title: UP Diliman Graduate Leads in Landscape Architects Licensure Exam
item.description: A graduate of the University of the Philippines-Diliman emerged as the top scorer in the computer-based licensure examination for landscape architects held earlier this month. According to the Profess...

item.title: Wildfires and smoke impact World Cup 2026 in Canada and the US
item.description: Wildfires across Canada have caused severe air quality issues, with Toronto recording the worst air pollution in the world. Over 1.9 million hectares of land have been burned, prompting evacuations an...

item.title: EU Mandates Google to Share Search Data and Open Android for Rival AI Companies
item.description: The European Union has imposed new regulations requiring Google to share anonymized search data with rival companies and open up its Android platform to allow alternative AI assistants to function on ...

item.title: Canadian Red Cross launches flood relief campaign for Ottawa residents
item.description: The Canadian Red Cross has initiated a fundraising appeal to support residents impacted by the 2026 Ottawa floods. This campaign aims to provide essential aid, including temporary housing, food securi...

item.title: Sen. Tommy Tuberville Faces Criticism After Claiming Some U.S. Senators Were Not Legally Elected Without Providing Evidence
item.description: Sen. Tommy Tuberville (R-Ala.) sparked widespread criticism after claiming during an interview on Newsmax that four or five current U.S. senators 'didn't legally win' their elections and therefore sho...
